title: Nvidia device not work in container created through API
body:
### Issue Description

Reading https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23560 it seems to say that

```
url -XPOST --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --request POST \
  --data '{"name":"test-container","image":"docker.io/nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04","devices":[{"path":"nvidia.com/gpu=all"}],"cmd":["sleep", "100000"]}' \
  --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock \
  -v 'http://d/v5.0.0/containers/create'
```

Should create the equivalent container as:

```
podman run  --rm --device nvidia.com/gpu=all -it nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04 nvidia-smi
```

Just with a different run command.

However that does not seem to be the case.

While the direct podman invocation nvidia-smi succeeds from me on a machine with the nvidia drivers installed.

Creating a container through the API, starting it and then using exec to enter the container started through the API and then trying to run nvidia-smi does not.

This would seem to indicated that adding the devices through the API does not result in the same thing as --device nvidia/com/gpu=all on the command line.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```text
user1@fedora:~/newpull/podman-desktop-extension-ai-lab$ podman run  --rm --device nvidia.com/gpu=all -it nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04 nvidia-smi
Wed Nov 27 03:04:28 2024

| NVIDIA-SMI 565.57.01              Driver Version: 565.57.01      CUDA Version: 12.7     |
|-----------------------------------------+------------------------+----------------------+
| GPU  Name                 Persistence-M | Bus-Id          Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |
| Fan  Temp   Perf          Pwr:Usage/Cap |           Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |
|                                         |                        |               MIG M. |
|=========================================+========================+======================|
|   0  NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 ...    Off |   00000000:07:00.0 Off |                  N/A |
|  0%   36C    P8              8W /  285W |       1MiB /  16376MiB |      0%      Default |
|                                         |                        |                  N/A |
+-----------------------------------------+------------------------+----------------------+
                                                                                         
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Processes:                                                                              |
|  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                              GPU Memory |
|        ID   ID                                                               Usage      |
|=========================================================================================|
|  No running processes found                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+



user1@fedora:~/newpull/podman-desktop-extension-ai-lab$ cat dopost.sh 
curl -XPOST --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --request POST \
  --data '{"name":"test-container","image":"docker.io/nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04","devices":[{"path":"nvidia.com/gpu=all"}],"cmd":["sleep", "100000"]}' \
  --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock \
  -v 'http://d/v5.0.0/containers/create'

user1@fedora:~/newpull/podman-desktop-extension-ai-lab$ bash dopost.sh 
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, POST is already inferred.
* processing: http://d/v5.0.0/containers/create
*   Trying /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to d (/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) port 80
> POST /v5.0.0/containers/create HTTP/1.1
> Host: d
> User-Agent: curl/8.2.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-Type: application/json
> Content-Length: 149
>

< Server: Libpod/4.9.4 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc000e14000
< Date: Wed, 27 Nov 2024 03:05:52 GMT
< Content-Length: 88
< 
{"Id":"553efe035d20ecef26f2981f82fdd79eac9bc237445a32679705aea7ea974e85","Warnings":[]}
* Connection #0 to host d left intact
user1@fedora:~/newpull/podman-desktop-extension-ai-lab$ podman start 553efe035d20ecef26f2981f82fdd79eac9bc237445a32679705aea7ea974e85
WARN[0000] Requested oom_score_adj=0 is lower than the current one, changing to 200 
553efe035d20ecef26f2981f82fdd79eac9bc237445a32679705aea7ea974e85
user1@fedora:~/newpull/podman-desktop-extension-ai-lab$ podman exec -it 553efe035d20ecef26f2981f82fdd79eac9bc237445a32679705aea7ea974e85 /bin/bash
root@553efe035d20:/# nvidia-smi
bash: nvidia-smi: command not found
```


### Describe the results you received

The nvidia devices is not available in the container started with the API

### Describe the results you expected

Adding devices through the API should allow access to the nvidia device.

comments.body:
First of all we only support the latest version upstream so I suggest you update especially as f39 is EOL now.

> http://d/v5.0.0/containers/create

You seem to be calling the docker API endpoint, the payload is for the libpod api http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create.
I don't know if the docker client supports the CDI format and if how they send it over the wire.

@Luap99 thanks for the info I'll try the libpod endpoint instead.

I'm on f39 as I'm not sure the nvidia drivers support later versions of Fedora. I'll see what it takes to install a newer version of podman on f39.

@Luap99 thanks for pointing out I was using the wrong endpoint. It works as expected with:

user1@fedora:~/newpull/podman-desktop-extension-ai-lab$ cat dopost2.sh 
curl -XPOST --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --request POST \
  --data '{"name":"test-container","image":"docker.io/nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04","devices":[{"path":"nvidia.com/gpu=all"}],"command":["sleep", "100000"]}' \
  --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock \
  -v 'http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create'


Unfortunately the example I copied in https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html does not have the libpod endpoint in the example (the example also does not seem to work if I change to the libpod endpoint, so I guess maybe its on purpose showing that you need to use one endpoint or the other depending on what you are calling?).
Closing as works as expected with the right endpoint.
